title: About
text: I’m Sajjad Sharifi. I’m based in the Boston area, working on urban informatics, broadly, applying data and spatial analysis to understand cities, with particular interest in fragile, conflict-affected, and post-displacement urban contexts.

title: About
text:
What I work on
Service accessibility, urban form under stress, population-displacement patterns, and the ways spatial data can support more grounded decision-making in fragility settings.

title: About
text:
How I work
Python (geopandas, osmnx, folium, rasterio), PostGIS, QGIS, and an increasing amount of R for spatial statistics. I build reproducible pipelines so analysis can be re-run, extended, and challenged.

title: About
text:
Tech stack

Languages: Python, R, SQL
Spatial: PostGIS, geopandas, osmnx, rasterio, QGIS
Visualization: Folium, matplotlib, Quarto
Infrastructure: A small client-server setup of my own — a Mac Mini hub running PostgreSQL/PostGIS and a MacBook Pro for analysis, connected privately via Tailscale.

title: Projects
text:
Future work
More projects will appear here over time, urban network analysis, transit equity, and other topics in urban informatics.

title: Sajjad Sharifi
text:
I work at the intersection of data and the urban realities of fragile and post-conflict contexts. Using spatial analysis, open data, and modern Python tooling, I try to surface the kinds of patterns, where services are, where people are, where the gap is widest, that practitioners and policymakers can actually act on.
This site is a small, growing collection of my work.

title: Sajjad Sharifi
text:
Featured project






Note→ Atlas of Urban Fragility: Four Cities, One Methodology



A comparative analysis of service-population alignment in Cox’s Bazar, Kabul, Mosul, and Goma, using open data and a consistent spatial methodology to surface where the gaps are widest in four very different fragile contexts.

title: Sajjad Sharifi
text:
Get in touch
I’m always glad to talk about urban informatics, fragility, or any of the projects below. Find me on GitHub or via the about page.
